texto: I walked back into the bedroom and, after all that, I actually was surprised. She lay on the bed, her hands nonchalantly behind her head, with the banana between her legs. Only half of it was alfresco. It was if we'd had sex and then, before heading for the bathroom, I'd bookmarked her vagina so as not to lose my place.

frase_original: I walked back into the bedroom and, after all that, I actually was surprised. She lay on the bed, her hands nonchalantly behind her head, with the banana between her legs. Only half of it was alfresco. It was if we'd had sex and then, before heading for the bathroom, I'd bookmarked her vagina so as not to lose my place.

texto: Anyway, my writer gang: they kind of did their comedy apprenticeship with me and, during that period, when they were young and impressionable, I think I infected them with my pun virus. They grew to enjoy puns, think puns, just as much as me. The problem is people don't really like puns any more, so I worry I've rendered the poor fuckers virtually unemployable.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Frank Skinner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Anyway, my writer gang: they kind of did their comedy apprenticeship with me and, during that per…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, comedy, humour pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e humour.

texto: Cider was my drink because I liked the taste and it made me stupid.
